L1.area: Object storage gateway and container file management logic

L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists where local cache files and directories are created with overly permissive access rights, and file permissions are only restricted after content has been written. This creates a time-of-check-time-of-use window in which a local attacker can read sensitive storlet or dependency data before access controls are applied.

L3.area: Docker-based storlet gateway and its local cache file provisioning logic
L3.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the Docker container gateway's file caching mechanism where storlet and dependency files are written to disk with overly permissive default permissions. File access restrictions are applied only after the content has already been written, creating a TOCTOU window that allows a local attacker to read the file contents before the permissions are tightened.

ground_truth.reason: Local cache files and directories for storlets and dependencies are created with world-readable permissions (0o755), and file-level permission restrictions are applied only after content is written, creating a TOCTOU race window for local attackers to read sensitive data.
